novel_features.name: Transcript grep
novel_features.description: Search inside actual episode transcripts, not just titles and descriptions.
novel_features.rationale: Downloads the transcripts[] URLs the API never indexes and full-text-searches them locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants what was *said* in episodes, not show metadata.

novel_features_built.name: Transcript grep
novel_features_built.description: Search inside actual episode transcripts, not just titles and descriptions.
novel_features_built.rationale: Downloads the transcripts[] URLs the API never indexes and full-text-searches them locally.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants what was *said* in episodes, not show metadata.

narrative.headline: The only PodcastIndex tool that is agent-native and works offline: search, resolve, and analyse any podcast from one signed binary with a local mirror you can FTS and SQL.
narrative.value_prop: Every existing PodcastIndex tool is a language SDK that mirrors the endpoints and returns raw JSON in-process. This CLI adds a local SQLite mirror, full-text search over synced shows and episodes, agent-native output, and compound commands no wrapper has: tgrep searches inside transcripts, cadence and dead-watch reason over publish history, guest-graph joins people across feeds.
narrative.auth_narrative: PodcastIndex signs every request with four headers: X-Auth-Key, X-Auth-Date (unix now), Authorization (sha1 of key+secret+date), and User-Agent. Set PODCASTINDEX_KEY and PODCASTINDEX_SECRET and the CLI computes the signature for you on each call.

quickstart.comment: verify the signer and credentials without hitting the API
resolve a show to its recent episodes

troubleshoots.fix: ensure PODCASTINDEX_KEY and PODCASTINDEX_SECRET are exported; run doctor to check clock skew on X-Auth-Date

troubleshoots.symptom: empty results on a known term
troubleshoots.fix: drop --clean or add --fulltext; the API truncates descriptions unless --fulltext is set

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs to discover podcasts, resolve a show to its episodes, or analyse podcast metadata, publish cadence, guests, value4value splits, or transcript content from the PodcastIndex database. It is the right tool when offline reuse, full-text search, or cross-feed analysis matters.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to download or play audio files; it returns enclosure URLs, not media.
Do not use it to add or manage feeds in PodcastIndex; the add/* endpoints need publisher auth and are out of scope.
Do not use it for Apple Podcasts-only metadata beyond the iTunes lookup passthrough.

recipes.title: Resolve a show in one step
recipes.explanation: Get the feedId for a show by name, ready to pass to episodes byfeedid.

recipes.title: Catch up on a show
recipes.explanation: List a show's newest episodes with just the fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Search inside transcripts
recipes.explanation: Full-text search the actual transcript files of a show, not just metadata.

trigger_phrases: resolve this show to its episodes
